32769.ToolTip: Used in some attacks and mini wall splats.

32773.ToolTip: Shakes camera based on given parameter

32775.ToolTip: Used when opponent lands on walls. Same as 0x8002

32776.ToolTip: Used when the attack hits. Same as 0x8003

32777.ToolTip: Gives a heavy camera shake. Same as 0x8004

32778.ToolTip: Shakes camera based on given parameter

32779.ToolTip:
Adds a ground ripple effect when triggered.
e.g Used when the player hits the ground.

32782.ToolTip: Used in some throws. Can use 0, 1 or 2.

32783.ToolTip: Adds a medium ground ripple effect when triggered.

32784.ToolTip: Adds a heavy ground ripple effect when triggered.

32791.ToolTip:
Used for effects such as hitsparks, Kazuya's forcefield VFX
Note: parameter is a decimal value converted from (VFX value + Limb value) in hex).
Ex: 11665421 (an example for a left hand heat engager) is B2000D in hex.
(B2 is the VFX hex, 000D is the limb the VFX will play on)
Some effects may not play on the limb or might have a set spot to play. 
VFX hex values in notes.

32795.ToolTip: Plays the little aura/spark VFX when Heat Smash is initiated

32804.ToolTip:
Looks like it's used to stop the VFXs played by prop 0x8049.
Value 125 and 126 spotted

32805.ToolTip: Plays an effect.  Follow the notes in 0x8017 and convert the values into decimal

32817.ToolTip:
Adds a Character specific hit spark. 
The value dictates the bone where the value will play from.
e.g Used alot in Throws or Ki Charging

32825.ToolTip:
Used for trail effects left behind by some characters' moves such as Jun's heal, etc.
Parameter is VFX ID. Jun's heal uses 793

32838.ToolTip: Used for stage effects such as the ground and water impacts.

32841.ToolTip:
Used for enabling Homing VFX
Note: parameter is a decimal value converted from (Limb value + VFX value) in hex).
Ex: The parameter for Heihachis yellow spark is 2496 in decimal
(2496 = 9C0 in hex, 9 = right hand, C0 = Yellow sparks value)
Some effects may not play on the limb or might have a set spot to play. 
VFX hex values in notes

32968.ToolTip: Used in cancels where input happens prior to cancel frames

32988.ToolTip:
Used whenever the game makes Kazuya transform into his devil state.

32996.ToolTip: Used in cancels where input happens prior to cancel frames

33008.ToolTip: Seen in Kazuya to perform some of his story attacks

33059.ToolTip:
Used whenever the game makes Kazuya transform into his devil state.
0 = Human form 1 = Devil form

33064.ToolTip:
Activates flags in player's structure. Each flag is 2 bytes.
Value format is 0xAAAABBBB
AAAA is Flag # - 0 - 99
BBBB is the value of the flag - 0 - 65535
Req 365 checks value of the flag.
0x60001 means activate flag 0x0006 and put value 0x0001 in it.

33081.ToolTip:
Related to showing/hiding Heat Meter.
Value 1 was causing the meter to disappear.

33094.ToolTip: Inflicts recoverable damage to the player at specified frame

33098.ToolTip: Offsets raven clone spawn position by value

33099.ToolTip: Parameter is how much recoverable health the player gains.

33101.ToolTip:
Turns on heat related properties (?)
Allows heat dash on-hit reactions on the opponent (Needs more experimenting)

33105.ToolTip:
Activates Kazuya's devil form permanently
Used in Story & Arcade modes to give you Devil Kazuya

33260.ToolTip:
Allows blocking during a reaction animation.
0 = Can't block. 1 = Allowed to block

33261.ToolTip:
Used in Ki-charge moves. 
The parameter is how many frames until the CH effect will be active.
e.g, For Jin's charge, value is 330 (frames)

33265.ToolTip:
While a throw, disconnects position of the characters. 
Removes mutual collision near walls

33269.ToolTip: While a throw, disconnects height average of the characters (during a few frames)

33270.ToolTip: While a throw, disconnects height average of the characters (during one frame)

33274.ToolTip:
Adds tracking properties to a move.
0 = No Tracking 1 = Homing Level Tracking 7 = Standard Tracking 12 = Infinite Tracking
e.g Used in a handful of homing moves.

33276.ToolTip: Can tweak the Player's Status. You can cross reference your state with the Frame Display

33277.ToolTip:
While a throw, brings back characters' collision.
Requires constant performance up to the end of the move (start frame must be 0x4XXX)

33278.ToolTip:
4096 = 100.0% size, Collision enabled
0 = 0% size, Collision disabled
Parameter is percentage written in hex form. E.g, 4096 is 0x1000 which is 100.0%

33325.ToolTip:
Gives a move Power Crush properties. This allows for a move to absorb hits.
Using it again with a value of 0 disables the power crush affect.
Value format is 0xAABBCCDD
AA - Powercrush flag (0 = Off, 1 = On), If this is Off, nothing gets absorbed
BB - Scaling applied to absorbed hits (100 = 100%)
CC - Lows & Throws flag (1 = absorb them, 0 = not absorb them)
DD - Player doesnt freeze when hit during power crush (0 = Off, 1 = On)

33326.ToolTip: Used in Rage Arts to trigger the Tint that's displayed when a RA is activated

33336.ToolTip: Value is the number of frames to skip

33337.ToolTip: Same as above but adds to the current frame counter

33338.ToolTip: Same as above but removes from the current frame counter

33339.ToolTip: Value is number of frame to skip, will be additioned with 0x1000

33340.ToolTip: Value is number of frame to skip, will be additioned with 0x2000

33343.ToolTip: Value is number of frame to skip, will be additioned with 0x4000

33344.ToolTip: Value is number of frame to skip, will be additioned with 0x8000

33348.ToolTip: Forces the opponent to play a move from the character's list of moves.

33349.ToolTip: Same as 0x8244. Don't know the difference b/w both yet

33350.ToolTip:
Slows down the player's attack. (4096 = 100%)
e.g Balancing moves to make them come out slower, etc.

33351.ToolTip:
Takes both positive and negative values. It's a modifier of some sort.
It doesn't add the value directly, multiplies it first with a float that we yet know nothing about.

33352.ToolTip: Same as 0x8246 except it's for opponent

33355.ToolTip:
Used in charged moves. Value indicates the speed the player will be set at when the move is charging.
This is needed for charge moves!

33356.ToolTip: Changes current animation with animation referenced by move id

33361.Function:
Horizontal Animation Distance Multiplier
(Eased Horizontal Movement)
33361.ToolTip:
Modifies how far the character moves forwards or backwards as a result of the animation. 
Value of 0 gives no horizontal movement. 
100 is default, using 200 would double the distance.
Value 110 means 10% increase in distance

33362.Function:
Vertical Animation Distance Multiplier
(Eased Vertical Movement)
33362.ToolTip:
Modifies how far the character moves upwards or downwards as a result of the animation. 
Value of 0 gives no vertical movement. 
100 is default
Origin of animation is within the floor

33364.ToolTip:
THE FLOOR GOES BOOM
Breaks the floor at a specified frame.
Depending on your current vulnerability will depend on what falling state will play when the floor breaks.

33368.ToolTip: Pushes player on world axis using specified pushback

33370.ToolTip: Sets pushback direction of prop 0x8258 to be the character's forward direction

33371.ToolTip: Sets pushback direction of prop 0x8258 to be the character's backward direction

33373.ToolTip: Pushes opponent on world axis using specified pushback

33375.ToolTip: Sets pushback direction of prop 0x825d to be the character's forward direction

33376.ToolTip: Sets pushback direction of prop 0x825d to be the opponent's forward direction

33391.ToolTip:
Used in tandem with 0x8270 to adjust the speed of the slide
e.g Yoshimitsu's d3+4 slide uses 4294959096 (0xFFFF DFF8)

33392.ToolTip: Slides the player in direction set by value

33393.ToolTip: Slides the player in direction set by value on world axis

33394.ToolTip:
Negative value: Rotate right
Positive value: Rotate left
See formula above for values

33395.ToolTip:
Negative value: Rotate right
Positive value: Rotate left
See formula above for values

33397.ToolTip:
From character's current positon, pushes your character into foreground or background.
0x1000 = 1.0 distance in frame data display
Imagine your character standing in a T-pose, then param value:
Positive: into character's left. Negative: into character's right.

33398.ToolTip:
From character's current positon, pushes your character forward or backward.
0x1000 = 1.0 distance in frame data display
Imagine your character standing in a T-pose, then param value:
Positive: into character's left. Negative: into character's right.

33400.ToolTip:
From character's current positon, pushes your character up or down.
0x1000 = 1.0 distance in frame data display
Imagine your character standing in a T-pose, then param value:
Positive: into character's left. Negative: into character's right.

33403.ToolTip: Spawns a projectile from the player. The Value is the ID of the Projectile

33405.Function:
Projectile Initial Horizontal Velocity
(Distance from Spawn Point)
33405.ToolTip:
Initial X-axis direction of the projectile. After unspecified number of frames, the projectile goes to the direction described within it.
E.g, Anna uses 880 for her PT1 projectile

33406.Function:
Projectile Initial Vertical Velocity
(Distance from Spawn Point)
33406.ToolTip: Initial Y-axis direction of the projectile. After unspecified number of frames, the projectile goes to the direction described within it. Anna's PT1 has value of 990 by default. Setting it to 3000 makes the Bazooka go upwards for a few frames and then in it's designated direction

33407.Function:
Projectile Initial Lateral Velocity
(Distance from Spawn Point)
33407.ToolTip:
Initial Z-axis direction of the projectile. After unspecified number of frames, the projectile goes to the direction described within it. Anna's PT1 has value of 205 by default. Setting it to 1000 makes the Bazooka go to the opponent's right for a few frames and then in it's designated direction.
Positive value: Foreground. Negative value: Background

33419.ToolTip:
REQUIRED FOR TEKKEN 8 STYLE THROWS.
Value indicates the maximum damage a scaled throw can do.
Starting frame should always be 0x8001

33420.ToolTip:
Damages the opponent when it reaches the specified frame. 
If other scale values are present the value indicates how strong it is in comparison to the other scale values.
Setting the value to 0 will deal the left over scaled damage. (Used at the end of throws)
100 Deals the maximum damage the throw can do.
DOESN'T WORK IF 0x828b IS NOT PRESENT BEFOREHAND

33421.ToolTip:
Damages the player when it reaches the specified frame. 
Same properties as 0x828c
DOESN'T WORK IF 0x828b IS NOT PRESENT BEFOREHAND

33430.ToolTip:
Value affects the character's jump height
Value Format: 0xXXYYZZZZ
XX - Upwards Vector
YY - Gravity
ZZZZ - Vertical Offset

33445.ToolTip:
Value is the percentage of the move's actual damage (assigned in the Hit Condition) inflicted on the opponent. Value 335 is required to cause 100% damage if the opponent has Rage because it activates Chip Damage Reduction buff.
Prop to be used on the attack, rather than on the reaction

33468.ToolTip:
Parameter tells at what frame the combo meter will be reset.
Found in reaction animations e.g, reaction to King's hopkick

33470.ToolTip: Can be used only on Reaction animations (?)

33473.ToolTip:
Makes the player invisible at any frame / value.
If value is 4, you will be invisible on frame 4,8, 12, 16 etc.

33474.ToolTip:
Makes the opponent invisible at any frame / value.
If value is 4, they will be invisible on frame 4,8, 12, 16 etc.

33492.ToolTip:
Yoshimitsu's katana in the left hand.
1 = Appears, 0 = Disappears

33493.ToolTip:
Yoshimitsu's wakizashi in the right hand.
1 = Appears, 0 = Disappears

33496.ToolTip:
Visual effect on Yoshimitsu's katana in the left hand.
1 = Appears, 0 = Disappears

33497.ToolTip:
Visual effect on Yoshimitsu's wakizashi in the right hand.
1 = Appears, 0 = Disappears

33504.ToolTip: Activates Rage if Rage is available.

33506.ToolTip: Removes rage (1). Allows rage to be obtained again (0)

33520.ToolTip:
Normally, animations force your character's neck/face to be in opponent's direction. This prop disables forcing. 
0 (OR ANY EVEN VALUE) = Disable
1 (OR ANY ODD VALUE) = Enable

33532.ToolTip:
Anything effected by this doesnt move. Including characters and the enivornment.
Value format is 0xYYYYZZZZ
YYYY - Game speed %
ZZZZ - How many frames will the slow down effect last for
Rage Art uses value 65566

33538.ToolTip:
Plays the 2D stop motion effect on player.
Value format is 0xWWXXYY??
?? is unknown.
WW is delay. When this property activates, how many frames to wait until character vibrates with YY intensity
XX is how many frames the character will stay stopped.
YY is the intensity of character vibration

33539.ToolTip:
Plays the 2D stop motion effect on opponent.
Value format is same as above

33549.ToolTip:
Different values control the HD Display.
The value format is 0xAAAABBBB

33555.ToolTip:
Required alongside 0x8314 to trigger intro/outro camera
Value indicates the type of cinematic. 1 = Intro, 2 = Outro

33556.ToolTip:
Required alongside 0x8313 to trigger intro/outro camera
Value is the intro index (0 - 2)

33592.ToolTip:
Used to add fake frame advantage to a move. The move to which this property is attached, imagine the last cancel says it goes back to neutral on frame 72. Then if this prop has a value of 72 as well, it will show you +1 on block.
Last Cancel's Frame + 1 - Prop value = Frame advantage.
Do note that the opponent's reaction animation length also plays into the calculation, exactly how is unknown

33593.ToolTip:
Used in Heat Bursts, which has 62 startup frames to show them as i16.
Param was 4294967250 which translates to -46.
62 - 46 = 16

33627.ToolTip:
Used to allow Leroy using his once-per-match cane move
0 = Not spend, 1 = Spend Leroy's cane install

33667.ToolTip:
Partially refills Heat Gauge based on value (1000 = 100%)
For draining Heat, use 4294967295 - (amount to drain)
Negative value drains Heat
Plays Heat activation VFX + SFX if it's not already active
Value 0 plays the Heat Deactivation SFX. Also sometimes uses Parameter 2 with Value 1

33671.ToolTip:
Removes recoverable health of the opponent.
Value of 200 spotted

33678.ToolTip:
Seen in Rage Arts
Handled through UE rather than movesets

33679.ToolTip:
Changes stage background to those seen in Rage Arts
E.g, 2 = Jun's RA background. 6 = Dragunov

33709.ToolTip:
Plays subtitles at a given frame.
Value format is 0xYYYYZZZZ
YYYY is subtitle number. E.g, 0x25 = "I will believe in my heart!"
ZZZZ is duration for which the subtitle will be displayed (in frames)
Subtitles values are universally stored (So Lars subtiles will load on Jin for example)

33713.ToolTip:
0 = Install consumed, 1 = Install activated
Seen in Bryan, Asuka, Leo etc.

33726.ToolTip:
Used in Heat Bursts to Pause/Resume Heat Timer
Value format 0xAAAABBBB
Any non-zero value at 0xAAAA pauses the timer. Duration uncontrolled
Any non-zero value at 0xBBBB doesn't let the timer be paused

33728.ToolTip:
Used to play VFX on moves where the recoverable health is removed from the opponent.
Uses param values 0, 35 and 4 (for Heihachi)
Value 1: needs to be 0
Value 2: duration of the VFX in frames
Valud 3: Unknown, probably VFX ID because changing value crashed the game

33738.ToolTip:
Can give a blue-ish glow to a character, one similar to Rage Drives in T7. Value format is 0xAABBCCDD
AA - 1 or 2
BB - VFX ID(?)
CC - Duration (?)
DD - Unknown, spotted to be zero
Spotted in Kazuya, Heihachi, Azazel and True Devil Kazuya

33742.ToolTip: Used in hit reactions to add scaling to followup attacks. Value is % scaling

33750.ToolTip: Affects everything: The characters, the stage props and animationsetc..

33751.ToolTip:
Anything effected by this doesnt move. Including characters and the enivornment.
Value format is 0xYYYYZZZZ
YYYY - Game speed %
ZZZZ - How many frames will the slow down effect last for
Used in Moves like Eddy d/b+4,4 (value: 327700)

33774.ToolTip:
Used in Eddy to handle his installs
Also spotted in Heihachi

33785.ToolTip:
Used in Heihachi to trigger his Warrior Instinct.
0 = Remove Warrior Instinct (removed the logo from HUD)
1 = Activate Warrior Instinct

33931.ToolTip: Attaches an Item onto the Player. Shotgun uses Value 30?

33932.ToolTip: Detaches an Item from the Player. Shotgun uses Value 30?

34133.ToolTip:
Seen in Boss Heihachi to restore his recoverable health.
Value format appears to be 0xAAAABBBB
Values seen: 0x000C0001 and 0x00190001

34314.ToolTip:
Plays the hand pose at the specified frame.
Value format is 0xAABB
AA - Index for Hand Pose
BB - Duration of blending into that pose

34315.ToolTip: Plays the hand pose for the left hand at the specified frame.

34316.ToolTip: Plays the hand pose for the right hand at the specified frame.

34318.ToolTip:
Blends both hands into their default poses (Left: #1, Right: #2).
Param is the blend duration in frames.
Use 0x860B/0x860C to override which pose each hand targets.

34322.ToolTip: It's suppose to play an animation but nothing was observed

34323.ToolTip: It's suppose to play an animation but nothing was observed

34436.ToolTip: It's suppose to play an animation but nothing was observed

34447.ToolTip: Parameter is the index of the Throw Camera to be played

34453.Function: Camera Zoom Focal Point / Camera transition
34453.ToolTip:
Value determines where the camera zooms at
0 = Zoom on P1, 1 = Zoom on P2, 2 = Zoom on both
Needs Extra Prop 0x8696 for it to work
It also has a unique param of 0xWWXXYYZZ in tandem with 0x8697 where
WW = The length of how long the camera will transition into place in frames
XX = The length of how long the camera will transition back to normal
YY = Will need more research (00 is spotted in tornado reacctions) (?)
ZZ = Focus point (00 = p1, 01 = p2, 02 = both p1 & p2) (?)

34454.ToolTip:
Camera zooms in momentarily
Use 0xAAWWXXYZ where
AA is how many frames it takes to reach zoomed-in position
WW is how long the camera stays zoomed in, X for how long it takes to zoom out, 
Y for the direction to zoom, Z for duration of zooming itself
e.g. 68420899 is mainly used for heavy hitting moves, 67765542 is commonly used for CH effects

34455.ToolTip:
Camera orbits momentarily
Use 0xWWXXYYZZ where
WW = Position of camera on the first axis
XX = Position of camera on the second axis
YY = How long the effect will last in frames
ZZ = How much zoom the effect will have
(Seen in tornado reactions)

34480.ToolTip: Used in Heat Bursts and Heat Engagers. Value controls the camera angle

34682.ToolTip: Parameter is the index of the facial animation to play.

34683.ToolTip:
Refer to prop 0x87f8
Drama = Intro / Outro / Fate

34684.ToolTip:
Used to expand muscles and make the body reddish. This uses atleast 2 values where each value is further divided into 2 sub-values. First value is about Fade-in and the 2nd one is about Fade-out.
0xAAAABBBB.
AAAA = For value-1, it is set 1 to Trigger it. For value-2, it's very likely the duration after which Fade-out will trigger.
BBBB = Duration of the Fade-in & Fade-out.

34685.ToolTip:
Same as above
Seen in Kazuya's EWGF

34799.ToolTip:
Requires 3 param values
1st Param - The folder of sounds
2nd Param - Unknown, is usually zero     
3rd Param - Actual voiceclip ID to be played

34808.ToolTip:
Used for playing dialogue in intros/winposes, both generic and character specific. Searches for appropriate voiceclip & facial anim inside the "dialogues" array within the moveset
Value format is 0xAAAABBBB
AAAA - Type of move. 0 = Intro, 1 = Outro, 2 = FATE Intro
BBBB - ID
E.g, All of Kazuya's "Enough of this farce" intro variations have the AAAA value as 0 and BBBB value as 2, which means "Intro # 2" (counting from 0)
